title: RLQ Pain - Acute Appendicitis
chief_complaint: Abdominal pain that started around umbilicus 12 hours ago, now shifted to right lower quadrant. Nausea, anorexia, low-grade fever.

physical_exam.abdomen: Tenderness at McBurney point, rebound tenderness, Rovsing sign positive, psoas sign positive

hidden_data.us_abd: Non-compressible tubular structure in RLQ, 8mm diameter with surrounding fluid, appendicolith present

key_learning_points: McBurney point tenderness = most reliable sign
Ultrasound first in young/thin patients, CT if unclear

patient_responses.pain_start: It started around my belly button last night, now it's on the right side
patient_responses.appetite: I don't want to eat anything, feel sick

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Classic appendicitis with migration of pain.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is acute appendicitis. The RLQ pain + leukocytosis + ultrasound confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Appendicitis: Laparoscopic appendectomy within 24h. Preoperative antibiotics (cefoxitin or cefazolin + metronidazole). Non-operative management with antibiotics for selected uncomplicated cases. Good outcome with early surgery.
